verses.text: The LORD: speak unto Israel, take a rod of every tribe — twelve rods, every man's name on his rod, Aaron's name on the rod of Levi. Lay them up in the tabernacle before the testimony. The rod of the man whom I choose shall blossom — I will make their murmurings to cease. Guidance: God settles the priesthood question with dry wood. No more debate, no more election by acclaim — let the dead stick speak. The man God chooses, He grows.

No more debate, no more election by acclaim — let the dead stick speak. The man God chooses, He grows.

The rod of the man whom I choose shall blossom — I will make their murmurings to cease. Guidance: God settles the priesthood question with dry wood. No more debate, no more election by acclaim — let the dead stick speak. The man God chooses, He grows.

The LORD: speak unto Israel, take a rod of every tribe — twelve rods, every man's name on his rod, Aaron's name on the rod of Levi. Lay them up in the tabernacle before the testimony. The rod of the man whom I choose shall blossom — I will make their murmurings to cease. Guidance: God settles the priesthood question with dry wood. No more debate, no more election by acclaim — let the dead stick speak. The man God chooses, He grows.

Moses laid the rods before the LORD in the tabernacle. On the morrow he went in — behold, Aaron's rod for the house of Levi was budded, brought forth buds, bloomed blossoms, and yielded almonds. Moses brought the rods out; every man took back his own. Guidance: One night. Dead almond wood became buds, blossoms, and ripe fruit at once. The eleven rods stayed wood. Calling shows itself not by argument but by fruit. God's chosen don't prove they're alive — they bloom.

Calling shows itself not by argument but by fruit. God's chosen don't prove they're alive — they bloom.

Moses brought the rods out; every man took back his own. Guidance: One night. Dead almond wood became buds, blossoms, and ripe fruit at once. The eleven rods stayed wood. Moses laid the rods before the LORD in the tabernacle. On the morrow he went in — behold, Aaron's rod for the house of Levi was budded, brought forth buds, bloomed blossoms, and yielded almonds. Moses brought the rods out; every man took back his own. Guidance: One night. Dead almond wood became buds, blossoms, and ripe fruit at once. The eleven rods stayed wood. Calling shows itself not by argument but by fruit. God's chosen don't prove they're alive — they bloom.

The LORD: bring Aaron's rod again before the testimony, to be kept as a token against the rebels — that thou mayest quite take away their murmurings from Me, that they die not. Moses did as the LORD commanded. Guidance: The blooming rod becomes a permanent exhibit in the ark — alongside the manna and the tablets. Three witnesses to who God is: He feeds, He commands, He chooses. The murmur silenced by a memorial, not just a moment.

The LORD: bring Aaron's rod again before the testimony, to be kept as a token against the rebels — that thou mayest quite take away their murmurings from Me, that they die not. Moses did as the LORD commanded. Guidance: The blooming rod becomes a permanent exhibit in the ark — alongside the manna and the tablets. Three witnesses to who God is: He feeds, He commands, He chooses. The murmur silenced by a memorial, not just a moment.

The children of Israel: behold, we die, we perish, we all perish. Whosoever cometh near the tabernacle of the LORD shall die — shall we all be consumed with dying? Guidance: Now they understand. After the earth opening, the fire, the plague, and the blooming rod, the awe finally lands: God is holy and we cannot just walk up. The chapter ends in trembling — the right response that the long rebellion had been avoiding. Fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; here it dawns.
